claim: Ninth MCC measurement. The one scale * t_end corner never measured in Sessions 4-9: scale=0.5 (10x the v0 baseline), t_end=1.0 s. Ensemble per_rule_with_pool_confirm, n=40 balanced, calibration=10, seed=42 (simulator) / panel_seed=42. Result: MCC = 0.060.
caveats: Higher scale reduces stochastic noise on pool deviations, which may tighten the FP/TP separation. Whether that actually lifts MCC above v4's 0.229 is what this measurement checks.
scale=0.5 is the same scale the existing test_knockouts.py uses and is close to the biological cell.

If MCC > 0.3 here, the path forward is running this config at full 458-CDS scope (~2 h wall on the same machine). If MCC <= 0.2, the architectural diagnosis from Session 9 also holds at higher scale - simulator biology is the bottleneck.
